---
title: "PK Analysis"
subtitle: "Introduction to `{ospsuite}`"
module: "Introduction to `{ospsuite}`"
ID: A09
date: last-modified
institute: |
  ![](../resources/esqlabs.svg){height=100}
format:
  esqlabs-revealjs:
    scrollable: true
    code-overflow: scroll
---

::: {.r-fit-text}
## **🎯 Goal: Learn to compute pharmacokinetic parameters of a simulation**
:::


```{r setup, echo = FALSE}
#| output-location: default
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.showtext = TRUE, fig.align = "center")
```



## PK-Analysis View in PK-Sim

:::center-x
![](https://881660647-files.gitbook.io/~/files/v0/b/gitbook-legacy-files/o/assets%2F-LNl6UIiFP7k0sNQthlR%2F-LQJPSoNjgtDlxBl9HHq%2F-LOWQ5AI-r7-sKUIoPqE%2Fpk-analysis-20-17.jpg?generation=1541162017853367&alt=media)


Also available using `{ospsuite}` !
:::

## Available PK parameters depend on the simulation type

  
| Simulation Type | PK Parameters                              |
|-----------------|--------------------------------------------|
| All             | AUC, Cmax, Cend, tmax, Half-Life, MRT, ... |
| IV              | Vss, Vd, CL, Body clearance, ...           |
| Oral            | Vss/F, Vd/F, CL/F, Fraction absorbed, ...  |
| Multiple adm.   | AUC, Cthrough_dDi, ...                     |

:::center-x
Full list: [docs.open-systems-pharmacology.org](https://docs.open-systems-pharmacology.org/working-with-pk-sim/pk-sim-documentation/pk-sim-simulations#pk-parameters)
:::

:::notes
- MRT: Mean residence time
- Vss: Volume of distribution at steady state
- Vd: Volume of distribution
- CL: Clearance
- F: Bioavailability
- Cthrough: Trough concentration just before the i-th dose is administered
:::


## Overview

1. Compute PK parameters
2. Access PK parameters
3. Import and export
4. Customize PK parameters

# Compute PK Parameters

## Calculated from Simulation Results

- PK parameters can be calculated for all outputs of a simulation.
- Pass a `SimulationResults` object to `calculatePKAnalyses()`.


```r
pkAnalysis <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simulationResults)
```

## Get simulation results

```{r}
#| output-location: default
# Load the package
library(ospsuite)

# Load the simulation
sim <- loadSimulation(system.file(
  "extdata",
  "Aciclovir.pkml",
  package = "ospsuite"
))

# Run the simulation
simulationResults <- runSimulations(sim)[[1]]
```

## Calculate PK Parameters

```{r}
pkAnalysis <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simulationResults)
pkAnalysis
```

::: {.callout-note}
`calculatePKAnalyses()` returns a `SimulationPKAnalyses` R6 object.
:::

# Explore PK Parameters


## Available Output Paths & Parameters

```{r}
pkAnalysis$allQuantityPaths
```

. . .

```{r}
pkAnalysis$allPKParameterNames
```



## All Parameters for One Output Path

Use `allPKParametersFor()` to retrieve all PK parameters for a given output path.

```{r}
pkAnalysis$allPKParametersFor(
  quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)"
)
```

## One Parameter

Use `pKParameterFor()` to retrieve a single PK parameter for a given output path.

```{r}
oneParam <- pkAnalysis$pKParameterFor(
  quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)",
  pkParameter = "C_max"
)
oneParam
```

. . .

Each returned object is a `QuantityPKParameter` with fields `$name`, `$quantityPath`, `$dimension`, `$unit`, and `$values`.

```{r}
oneParam$values
```

## Get a Data Frame

`pkAnalysesToDataFrame()` returns all PK parameters for all output paths as a data frame.

```{r}
pkAnalysisDF <- pkAnalysesToDataFrame(pkAnalysis)
pkAnalysisDF[, c("QuantityPath", "Parameter", "Value", "Unit")]
```

::: {.callout-note}
Columns: `IndividualId`, `QuantityPath`, `Parameter`, `Value`, `Unit`.
:::

# Import and Export

## CSV Interoperability

- PK parameters can be exported and imported through `.csv` files.
- These files are interoperable between PK-Sim and `{ospsuite}`.

## Import

```{r}
#| output-location: default
# example file
PKAnalysisFilePath <- system.file(
  "extdata",
  "PKAnalyses.csv",
  package = "ospsuite"
)

# import to ospsuite framework
pkAnalysis <- importPKAnalysesFromCSV(
  filePath = PKAnalysisFilePath,
  simulation = sim
)
```

::: {.callout-warning}
The related simulation must be loaded to import the PK parameters.
:::

## Export

```{r}
#| output-location: default
exportPKAnalysesToCSV(pkAnalyses = pkAnalysis, filePath = "PKAnalysis.csv")
```

```{r, echo = FALSE}
#| output-location: default
read.csv("PKAnalysis.csv")

invisible(file.remove("PKAnalysis.csv"))
```


# User-Defined PK Parameters

## Custom PK parameters

- Useful when working on different time intervals or dimensions other than concentrations
- Based on existing PK parameters (listed in `ospsuite::StandardPKParameter`)
- Custom formulas are not supported

```r
addUserDefinedPKParameter(name, standardPKParameter)
```

## Sample of `StandardPKParameter`

`ospsuite::StandardPKParameter` exposes the available base parameters:

```{r}
#| output-location: default
names(ospsuite::StandardPKParameter)
```

## Create a New Parameter

```{r}
myAUC <- addUserDefinedPKParameter(
  name = "MyAUC",
  standardPKParameter = StandardPKParameter$AUC_tEnd
)

myAUC
```

## Time Interval

```{r}
# Specifies start time and end time in minutes
myAUC$startTime <- 50
myAUC$endTime <- 80

myAUC
```

## Application-Based Interval

For multi-dose protocols, restrict the interval to specific administrations using application indices and offsets instead of absolute times:

```r
myAUC$startApplicationIndex <- 2
myAUC$endApplicationIndex <- 3
myAUC$startTimeOffset <- 0     # minutes after start application
myAUC$endTimeOffset <- 0       # minutes after end application
```

## Calculate User-Defined PK Parameters

```{r}
pkAnalysis <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simulationResults)

myAUC_result <- pkAnalysis$pKParameterFor(
  quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)",
  pkParameter = "MyAUC"
)

myAUC_result
```

. . .

```{r}
myAUC_result$values
```



## Exercise Overview

Practice PK parameter analysis on the Aciclovir simulation:

- **Plasma PK.** Get `C_max` and `C_tEnd` for plasma concentrations.
- **Add whole blood output.** Repeat for whole blood concentrations after adding the output.
- **Alternate dose.** Build a 100 mg simulation and export its PK table.
- **Custom parameters.** Define time-bounded `C_max` / `C_min` and compute ratio.

## Baseline PK

Let's practice! Start by accessing and saving into an object the value of `C_max` and `C_tEnd` for the Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood).

```{r,  eval = TRUE}
#| output-location: default
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Solution"
library(ospsuite)

# Simulation Setup
simExercise <- loadSimulation(
  system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite"),
  loadFromCache = FALSE
)
simExercise_res <- runSimulations(simExercise)[[1]]

# We create the PK analysis
pkAnalysisExercise <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simExercise_res)

# And access our PK parameters of interest
C_maxExercise <- pkAnalysisExercise$pKParameterFor(
  quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)",
  pkParameter = "C_max"
)$values

C_tEndExercise <- pkAnalysisExercise$pKParameterFor(
  quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)",
  pkParameter = "C_tEnd"
)$values
```

## Whole Blood Concentration

Repeat the process but with the following output: `"Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Whole Blood (Peripheral Venous Blood)"`

```{r,  eval = TRUE}
#| output-location: default
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Solution"
# We do the same for another output of our choice, here the whole blood
# First, we need to add the output to the simulation, and re run it
addOutputs(
  "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Whole Blood (Peripheral Venous Blood)",
  simulation = simExercise
)
simExercise_res <- runSimulations(simExercise)[[1]]

pkAnalysisExercise <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simExercise_res)

C_maxExercise_wholeBlood <- pkAnalysisExercise$pKParameterFor(
  quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Whole Blood (Peripheral Venous Blood)",
  pkParameter = "C_max"
)$values

C_tendExercise_wholeBlood <- pkAnalysisExercise$pKParameterFor(
  quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Whole Blood (Peripheral Venous Blood)",
  pkParameter = "C_tEnd"
)$values
```


## 100 mg Simulation

Create a new simulation with a dose of 100 mg, run it, then create a data frame called `pkAnalysisDF_100mg` containing all PK parameters of the Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood). Export the PK analysis to a csv file.

```{r,  eval = TRUE}
#| output-location: default
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Reminder"
# Simulation Setup
simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite")

simDose100 <- loadSimulation(simFilePath, loadFromCache = FALSE)

Dose100 <- getParameter(
  "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose",
  simDose100
)
setParameterValues(Dose100, 100, units = "mg")

# We run the simulation
simDose100_res <- runSimulations(simDose100)[[1]]
```

## 100 mg PK Data Frame + Export

```{r,  eval = TRUE}
#| output-location: default
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Solution"
pkAnalysis_100mg <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simDose100_res)

# First, let's create the data frame and have a look at it
pkAnalysisDF_100mg <- pkAnalysesToDataFrame(pkAnalysis_100mg)
pkAnalysisDF_100mg

# Then, we export the PK parameters
# Note: we can do both things independently
exportPKAnalysesToCSV(
  pkAnalyses = pkAnalysis_100mg,
  filePath = "pkAnalysis_100mg.csv"
)
```

```{r, echo = FALSE}
#| output-location: default
invisible(file.remove("pkAnalysis_100mg.csv"))
```

## Custom C_max and C_min

Now, add two PK parameters, `C_max` and `C_min`, between 10 and 90 minutes, for the plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood). Then, compute the ratio `C_max/C_min` for the original simulation and for the new one with a dose of 100 mg.

```{r,  eval = TRUE}
#| output-location: default
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Solution"
myC_max <- addUserDefinedPKParameter(
  name = "myC_max",
  standardPKParameter = StandardPKParameter$C_max
)

myC_min <- addUserDefinedPKParameter(
  name = "myC_min",
  standardPKParameter = StandardPKParameter$C_min
)

myC_max$startTime <- 10
myC_max$endTime <- 90

myC_min$startTime <- 10
myC_min$endTime <- 90

pkAnalysisExercise <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simExercise_res)
pkAnalysis_100mg <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simDose100_res)
```

## Ratio — Original Simulation

```{r,  eval = TRUE}
#| output-location: default
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Solution"
# We compute the PK parameters for the original simulation
pk_cmax <- pkAnalysisExercise$pKParameterFor(
  quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)",
  pkParameter = "myC_max"
)

pk_cmin <- pkAnalysisExercise$pKParameterFor(
  quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)",
  pkParameter = "myC_min"
)

ratioExercise <- pk_cmax$values / pk_cmin$values

ratioExercise
```

## Ratio — 100 mg Simulation

```{r,  eval = TRUE}
#| output-location: default
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Solution"
# And for the new one
pk_cmax_100mg <- pkAnalysis_100mg$pKParameterFor(
  quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)",
  pkParameter = "myC_max"
)
pk_cmin_100mg <- pkAnalysis_100mg$pKParameterFor(
  quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)",
  pkParameter = "myC_min"
)

# The same ratio is obtained, but C_max and C_min values are different
ratio100mg <- pk_cmax_100mg$values / pk_cmin_100mg$values

ratio100mg
```


## Resources

- [PK Sim section about PK-Parameters](https://docs.open-systems-pharmacology.org/working-with-pk-sim/pk-sim-documentation/pk-sim-simulations#pk-parameters)
- [`{ospsuite}` vignette](https://www.open-systems-pharmacology.org/OSPSuite-R/articles/pk-analysis.html)
